sobrevivencia 0.0.6
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • turnbull

    augment method for ypreg (Yang & Prentice) models

    augment.ypreg.Rd

    augment method for ypreg (Yang & Prentice) models

    # S3 method for class 'ypreg'
augment(x, data = NULL, newdata = NULL, ...)

    Arguments

    x

    a fitted model object of class "ypreg".

    data

    a data frame to augment. Defaults to the model frame used during fitting (covariates only, response excluded). Ignored when newdata is supplied.

    newdata

    an optional data frame of new observations. When provided, both linear predictors and their standard errors are computed from these data. Because observed outcomes are not available, .time, .event, and all residual columns are set to NA.

    ...

    further arguments passed to or from other methods.

    Value

    A tibble::tibble() with one row per observation. The first two columns are:

    .time

    observed failure/censoring time;

    .event

    event indicator (1 = event, 0 = censored).

    Followed by the original covariate columns and:

    .fitted.short

    short-term linear predictor \(X\hat{\beta}_{\text{short}}\);

    .se.fit.short

    standard error of the short-term linear predictor;

    .fitted.long

    long-term linear predictor \(X\hat{\beta}_{\text{long}}\);

    .se.fit.long

    standard error of the long-term linear predictor;

    .resid.coxsnell

    Cox-Snell residuals, or NA when newdata is supplied;

    .resid.martingale

    martingale residuals, or NA when newdata is supplied;

    .resid.deviance

    deviance residuals, or NA when newdata is supplied.

    Examples

    library(survival)
library(survstan)
fit <- ypreg(Surv(futime, fustat) ~ age + rx, data = ovarian, dist = "weibull")

# Augment training data
augment(fit)
#> # A tibble: 26 × 11
#>    .time .event   age    rx .fitted.short .se.fit.short .fitted.long
#>    <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>
#>  1    59      1  72.3     1          18.7          9.76       -1.13 
#>  2   115      1  74.5     1          19.4         10.1        -1.16 
#>  3   156      1  66.5     1          17.0          8.85       -1.05 
#>  4   421      0  53.4     2          10.7          5.66       -1.08 
#>  5   431      1  50.3     1          12.3          6.36       -0.845
#>  6   448      0  56.4     1          14.1          7.30       -0.923
#>  7   464      1  56.9     2          11.7          6.16       -1.13 
#>  8   475      1  59.9     2          12.6          6.58       -1.17 
#>  9   477      0  64.2     1          16.3          8.50       -1.02 
#> 10   563      1  55.2     2          11.2          5.92       -1.11 
#> # ℹ 16 more rows
#> # ℹ 4 more variables: .se.fit.long <dbl>, .resid.coxsnell <dbl>,
#> #   .resid.martingale <dbl>, .resid.deviance <dbl>

# Predictions on new observations
augment(fit, newdata = data.frame(age = c(50, 60, 70), rx = c(1, 2, 1)))
#> # A tibble: 3 × 11
#>   .time .event   age    rx .fitted.short .se.fit.short .fitted.long .se.fit.long
#>   <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>
#> 1    NA     NA    50     1          12.2          6.31       -0.841        0.784
#> 2    NA     NA    60     2          12.6          6.60       -1.17         1.04 
#> 3    NA     NA    70     1          18.0          9.40       -1.10         1.20 
#> # ℹ 3 more variables: .resid.coxsnell <dbl>, .resid.martingale <dbl>,
#> #   .resid.deviance <dbl>
